input_row.title: Ensure 'Minimum Inbound TLS Version' is set to '1.2' or higher
input_row.impact: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services. 2. Click the name of an app. 3. Under Settings, click Configuration. 4. In the General settings pane, under Platform settings, click the drop-down next to Minimum Inbound TLS Version and select 1.2 or higher. 5. Click Save. 6. Click Continue. 7. Repeat steps 1-6 for each app requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI For each app requiring remediation, run the following command to update the TLS version: az webapp config set --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name <app-name> --min-tls-version <1.2|1.3> Remediate from PowerShell For each app requiring remediation, run the following command to update the TLS version: Set-AzWebApp -ResourceGroupName <resource-group-name> -Name <app-name> - MinTlsVersion <1.2|1.3> Default Value: By default, TLS version is set to 1.2. References: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/configure-ssl-bindings#how- can-i-change-the-minimum-tls-versions-for-the-app 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/mcsb-data- protection#dp-3-encrypt-sensitive-data-in-transit 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/mcsb-network- security#ns-8-detect-and-disable-insecure-services-and-protocols 4. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/webapp 5. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.websites/get-azwebapp 6. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.websites/set-azwebapp 7. https://csrc.nist.gov/news/2019/nist-publishes-sp-800-52-revision-2 CIS Controls:

gpt_response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control requires checking the "Minimum Inbound TLS Version" property that can be queried using Azure APIs.
- The audit mentions specific Azure CLI commands (`az webapp config set`) for checking and setting the TLS version.
- The control is related to standard security configurations for Azure App Services.
- Based on the above criteria, the control satisfies the conditions for an automated audit.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_app_service_minimum_tls_version_verified

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- The program would utilize Azure Resource Manager APIs to verify the Minimum Inbound TLS Version setting for Azure App Services.
- The specific Azure CLI command to use is: 
  ```
  az webapp config show --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name <app-name>
  ```
- It will enumerate all web applications under a specified resource group to check the TLS version.
- The configuration properties/settings to validate would include `minTlsVersion`.
- The clear Pass/Fail criteria would be:
  - **Pass**: The `minTlsVersion` value is set to `1.2` or `1.3`.
  - **Fail**: The `minTlsVersion` value is set to anything lower than `1.2` or is not set.
- An example API endpoint to query using Azure REST APIs would be:
  ```
  GET https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/{subscription-id}/resourceGroups/{resource-group-name}/providers/Microsoft.Web/sites/{app-name}/config/web?api-version=2021-02-01
  ```
